prose: Method–provenance block: Most electrophilic condensed Fukui function (f+) for nitrobenzene — DFT, tier ‘standard’ (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF  (gas phase), charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied XYZ (14 atoms) treated as fixed; the N±1 states (cation, anion) were single-point calculations at the same geometry (so the protocol is fukui(B3LYP)//geometry(input)). No implicit solvent; pure gas-phase continuum. SCF cycles for the N, N+1 and N–1 states all reported convergence.  Live engine output was streamed to a timestamped log (fukui_<timestamp>.out) in the call-time working directory; you can tail –f that file for the full SCF and Mulliken-charge transcript.  Convergence & warnings: the integrity checker flagged three warnings (verbatim below and in the warnings field). The integrity verdict is trustworthy: true.  Result: the carbon at atom index 3 (label 4, symbol C) is the most electrophilic site, with f+ = 0.49641782 (dimensionless).  Warnings (verbatim):  • sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  • sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  • common header present  Reproducibility: CLI invocation recorded in result JSON as   /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpr78487oc/live.json  The per-atom f+, f-, f0 arrays and the dual descriptor, plus a PNG plot of the Fukui maps, are written under   /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_o3/20260702-230507_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/ (see plot.png).
